intro_text: You're hunting alone when you notice an unfamiliar hollow under a tree. Was that always there? The opening is criss-crossed with spiderwebs, and you think you see insects skittering around inside.
decline_text: Nope, that's too creepy for you! You retreat to a different part of the territory  where you can hunt far away from the ominous, spiderwebbed gap.

success_outcomes.text: You crouch and pad into the hollow, clawing thick spiderwebs out of your way, but you stop when your paw hits something round and hard tangled up in the webbing. Though the webs cling to your paw and tangle in your fur, you manage to pull the mysterious object free. You're nervous about what you might find, but when you uncover it, you're surprised to find an unfamiliar orange... fruit? You roll it around, confused, but decide to take your discovery with you.

You creep into the hollow, brushing spiderwebs aside. The insects skitter away from you as you inspect the root-threaded walls. You can't find any hints about where this space came from, so you eventually leave and return to hunting, but you later find one of the insects hiding in your fur. Strangely enough, it seems to like you, so you decide to keep it.

You glance into the hollow, but your attention is captured by the spiderwebs themselves. They sway in the wind, some thin and some more like clumps of fur, some with dried leaves and petals caught in their strands. They're kind of creepy, but also strangely beautiful. You continue your patrol, considering ways you could decorate the camp with spiderwebs when you get back!

You clear away the spiderwebs and inspect the interior of the hollow. You're about to dismiss it as empty and leave when you notice something on the ground: small berries, shriveled almost to nothingness. Many cats would have no way to identify them, but you recognize these as very old deathberries and shiver. What could have happened here? You're left with more questions than answers, but with the satisfying suspicion that you've uncovered the corner of a long-hidden secret.

As you clear spiderwebs away from the hollow's entrance, you hear a soft gasp and meet the eyes of a translucent cat, curled up behind the webbing. {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject/CAP} {VERB/n_c:0/whisper/whispers} for you to continue, and when you've finally cleared the opening, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} cautiously {VERB/n_c:0/step/steps} out into the sunlight. {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject/CAP} {VERB/n_c:0/tell/tells} you that {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject}{VERB/n_c:0/'ve/'s} been trapped there for a long while, and that despite being a ghost, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} couldn't pass through the spiderwebs. The cat thanks you profusely, then leaves to enjoy {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} new freedom.

fail_outcomes.text: You carefully pad inside, sniffing around for any sign that an animal lives here. The spiderwebs suggest that the hollow is abandoned, but you can't be too careful! You quickly scan the walls for any hints of where this space came from, but besides some long scratches on the walls, you don't find any clues. Disappointed and a bit unnerved, you turn and leave.

You start to approach the hollow, but stop as cold air wafts from the opening. The air smells like crowfood and death, and everything about it feels wrong. You scramble back, your fur standing on end, and flee as quickly as you can.

You creep into the hollow and look around, clawing spiderwebs out of your way. Roots stick out of the walls, some of them covered in... scratch marks? You suddenly realize that the hollow has gotten darker, and turn to see that the spiderwebs have thickened. The pathway you ripped through them has completely disappeared, like they're sealing you in. Panic wells up in your chest and you dash the way you came, tearing through the webbing, and run until you're out of breath.

You creep into the hollow, clawing spiderwebs out of your way. The air inside is uncomfortably cold, and when it brushes against your fur a chill shoots down your spine. You think that the faint noise you hear is just wind, but as it grows louder, you realize it's your name, somehow echoing all around you: y_c. y_c! You flee, running as fast as you can until you're back at camp.

intro_text: You're on a dawn patrol with r_w_neurotic. Fog hangs over the whole territory, turning familiar landmarks into ghostly silhouettes and making it hard to see even several tail-lengths in front of you. Suddenly, r_w_neurotic gasps. With a jolt, you follow {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/poss} gaze and see an indistinct, cat-sized shadow with two glowing eyes, watching you. The figure turns and runs. A haunting jingling noise follows them.
decline_text: Absolutely not. It's too early for this! You turn away and lead r_w_neurotic the opposite direction.

success_outcomes.text: Despite r_w_neurotic's protests, you leap after the shadow. You quickly catch up to it and leap for it, but your paws pass straight through the shape and you crash to the ground! When you've finally regained your bearings, the shadow is gone. The only sign of it is a belled collar lying a tail-length away.

You run after the shadow. The fog makes it hard to smell, and the strange jingling noise seems to be coming from everywhere, but you've always had keen senses. You pinpoint the direction that the noise actually seems to be coming from and follow it. The sound gets clearer and closer until you burst out of the fog into a clearing. There's no figure in sight, but a collar lies on the ground, its bell glinting in the sudden sunlight.

You remember an old story that says dead cats sometimes appear when the fog is thickest: not StarClan cats, but cats who have died and now roam elsewhere. You call after the shadow, asking if they're one of those cats. For a long moment, there's no response. r_w_neurotic quivers beside you. Then, the figure reappears and blinks at you with its still-glowing eyes. You make out an indistinct cat silhouette before it seems to fade and become one with the fog, leaving a belled collar on the ground where it once stood.

You race after the jingling noise, and r_w_neurotic follows close behind. As you run, the fog gradually clears, until you're standing before a gnarled, lone tree with tangled chimes hanging from one branch. Was that what was making the noise? You're skeptical, but r_w_neurotic is obviously spooked, so you quickly leave the area.

You race after the jingling noise, and r_w_neurotic follows close behind. As you run, the fog gradually clears, until you're standing before a gnarled, lone tree with tangled chimes hanging from one branch. Was that what was making the noise? Despite r_w_neurotic's nervous protests, you climb the tree and cut the chimes down before heading back towards more familiar territory.

You're familiar with ghost cats, and that looks like a ghost! You gently call after the shadow, and {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/pause/pauses}. Slowly, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/pad/pads} back towards you, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} collar gently jangling. While the interaction is a bit awkward, especially with a clearly spooked r_w_neurotic beside you, you have a polite conversation before parting ways.

You're about to run after the shadow but r_w_neurotic, panicked, begs for you not to leave {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object}. {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject/CAP}{VERB/r_w_neurotic/'re/'s} wide-eyed and breathing hard, clearly terrified by the fog. You turn back and talk to {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} gently, urging {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} to take deep breaths, trying to calm {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} down. It takes a few minutes, but you manage to talk r_w_neurotic out of {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/poss} panic, and your Clanmate is shaken but grateful.

fail_outcomes.text: For a second, you're frozen, but then you leap after the shadow. Is it another cat? A harmless kittypet, or maybe a dangerous rogue? You're spurred onwards by a need to make sure the shadow isn't a threat to your Clan, but you lose it in the thick fog. It takes you a while to find your way back to r_w_neurotic.

You start to run after the shadow, but r_w_neurotic leaps in front of you, {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/poss} eyes wide and {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/poss} fur fluffed in terror. {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_w_neurotic/beg/begs} you not to follow the figure and {VERB/r_w_neurotic/plead/pleads} for you to come back to camp with {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} instead. You manage to talk {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} out of {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/poss} panic, but by then the shadow is long gone.

You leap after the figure, but you trip and slam into the ground. You look back to see what you tripped over, and see a familiar log... But you recognize it from a completely different part of the territory. How did you get here? Did the fog disorient you so much you've been wandering the complete wrong direction? Unnerved, and with the shadowy figure now long gone, you and r_w_neurotic quickly make your way back to camp.

You race after the shadow, but soon the fog's so thick you can barely see your own whiskers. You still hear that strange jingling sound, though, and try to follow it, but it seems to be coming from multiple directions at once. Suddenly, a shadowy paw shoots out of the fog and slashes at you! You dodge and prepare to fight back, but the shadow doesn't reappear.

You plunge into the fog, following the jingling sound. Soon, you can't see anything, and the jingling seems to be coming from all around you. You think you're running in circles, but without any visible landmarks, it feels like you're in a void. You're not sure how long it takes for the fog to clear. When you finally regain your bearings, you're at the edge of the territory, and you trudge home to find a distraught r_w_neurotic telling your Clanmates how the fog seemed to eat you.

intro_text: r_w_neurotic has been insisting that a grove of trees on the territory is haunted. {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_w_neurotic/claim/claims} to hear strange noises whenever {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject} {VERB/r_w_neurotic/get/gets} close, and {VERB/r_w_neurotic/say/says} {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject} {VERB/r_w_neurotic/feel/feels} like {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject}{VERB/r_w_neurotic/'re/'s} being watched. After several days of this, you consider checking the grove out yourself.
decline_text: You don't have time to investigate every rumor you hear. If r_w_neurotic is really worried, {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject} should ask someone to accompany {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/object} {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/self}.

success_outcomes.text: As you approach the grove, you hear a strange hissing noise. You crouch and creep forward, alert for any signs of danger. Could r_w_neurotic be right? Could the grove really be haunted? You follow the sound to a bundle of Twoleg trash and cautiously paw through it until you uncover a strange, hissing device. When you bat at the pieces sticking out of it, the hissing subsides, and you triumphantly return to camp with the now-silent object to let r_w_neurotic know what you discovered.

You approach the grove and hear a haunting, bird-like chorus coming from the branches above. You creep forward, ready to fight or flee, but... oh! You're familiar with ghost cats, but this is a different sight: a flock of translucent birds flits through the canopy, calling to each other with chirps that sound a bit too ghostly and resonant to be comforting. You're not sure how r_w_neurotic was able to hear them, too, but they don't look like a threat. You watch the ghost birds for a while before returning to camp.

As you enter the grove, something lunges at you with a yowl! You yelp and prepare to fight or flee, only for you to realize your attacker is... r_w_neurotic? It seems this was the setup to a prank! You wouldn't have guessed r_w_neurotic to be capable of such a thing, but {PRONOUN/r_w_neurotic/subject} {VERB/r_w_neurotic/seem/seems} thrilled to have scared you.

fail_outcomes.text: It's not long after you enter the grove that you notice a shape following you. It's huge and winged, but seems to vanish whenever you chase after it. You search for a while, your unease and frustration growing, until you finally leave. For a moment, you think you see scarlet eyes watching you leave, but surely that's a trick of the light...

When you arrive, the grove is uncomfortably silent. You pad through the undergrowth, listening for any strange sounds, but the only thing you notice is the growing sense that you're being watched. When the feeling gets too much for you to take, you turn tail and dash out of the grove, not stopping until you get home.

As you enter the grove, you hear a strange creaking noise coming from the canopy. You tense, but quickly realize it's just the trees creaking as they're blown back and forth by the wind. You spend some time searching the area for anything suspicious, but find nothing. You aren't convinced that r_w_neurotic wouldn't have recognized the sounds of creaking trees, but that must be all... right?

intro_text: While sniffing around near the border, you hear a kittypet call out to you. They've got some sort of weird Twoleg contraption on, and they ask for your help getting out of the thing.
decline_text: You pretend not to hear the kittypet and turn back towards camp. Whatever they're wearing, they don't seem to be in pain from it, so it's not your duty to help the stranger.

success_outcomes.text: You agree to help, and are able to wrestle the strange device over the kittypet's head, allowing {PRONOUN/n_c:0/object} to wriggle free. {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject/CAP} {VERB/n_c:0/tell/tells} you that if you don't take it with you back home, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} housefolk are just going to make {PRONOUN/n_c:0/object} wear it again. Weird. It can't be that uncomfortable, right?

You try with the kittypet for a few moments, but you're unable to get the weird thing off. Eventually, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/accept/accepts} {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} fate, saying {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} housefolk seem to quite enjoy the costume, so maybe {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} can put up with it until the end of the season. Those Twolegs sure have some strange practices.

You and the kittypet are able to work together to get the weird thing off, that {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/call/calls} a costume. Picking it up in {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} mouth, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/give/gives} you a thankful nod and {VERB/n_c:0/run/runs} off to go bury it where {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} Twolegs will never find it.

fail_outcomes.text: You approach the kittypet to try and help, but you hear the call of a Twoleg, and they roll their eyes and turn around, saying they need to get back home. What kind of cat lets a Twoleg boss them around?

As you get near the kittypet to help, they change their mind and turn you away, stating that you smell of herbs and Clan cats, and that's apparently a bad thing. Your tail twitches in irritation as they turn to go back to their Twoleg home. They'd better hope they don't find themselves in a position where they need help from those herbs and cats!

intro_text: You hear a strange bellowing noise from the forest late one night, after every other cat in camp has gone to sleep. But you're wide awake. You wonder if it's worth investigating.
decline_text: None of your business. You head back to your nest, putting a paw over your ears when you hear the eerie noise for a second time.

success_outcomes.text: You cautiously pick your way into the forest in the direction the noise came from. After some wandering around in the dark, you find a pristine deer skull, sitting perfectly on top of the stump of a tree that you could've sworn was standing just a few days ago. A bit freaked out, you head back to camp. The next morning, the skull is sitting right next to your nest, and your denmates are complimenting you on the cool find.
When you venture into the forest, you find a freshly fallen tree. Weird-- the night is perfectly still, why would a tree have fallen? It's a bit unsettling... maybe it's none of your business.

fail_outcomes.text: You head into the forest to find the source of the sound, but the cold night air pierces your fur, and you decide to head back home right away. You could've sworn it was only a few minutes of wandering, but when you get back to camp, you find the sun beginning to rise.

You approach the camp entrance, ready to investigate, but you hear the noise again, louder, and you suddenly have no interest in getting any close to it. You race back to your nest, cover your ears with moss, and hope you can go to sleep and forget about it.

intro_text: While on a lone border patrol, you notice a large dog pawing at a kittypet, its huge, fluffy tail lashing!
decline_text: You turn to head back to camp and warn your Clan about the dog. Hopefully that kittypet can fend for itself.

success_outcomes.text: You let out a yowl, attempting to taunt the dog and give the kittypet a chance to escape. However, the dog doesn't seem to care. Instead, the kittypet takes notice and tells you politely not to panic-- the dog is their friend, and they're just playing. How strange. They might as well try to befriend a forest fire!

You crawl forwards on your belly and quietly call out to the kittypet, asking if they need your help. They say no, and tell you that this dog is in fact their friend. Sensing your scepticism, they invite you to approach the dog, which you do with unsheathed claws. The dog gives you a curious sniff and a lick on the head before returning its focus to the kittypet. Why can't all dogs be so harmless?
You cautiously trot forward, wondering if the kittypet may be in danger. However, when you see the dog's face, you can tell that they're friendly, and just playing. What a cute couple of friends!

fail_outcomes.text: You launch yourself forwards, jumping onto the dog's back and sinking your claws in, yowling at the kittypet to run while they can. However, the dog just yelps pathetically and drops to the ground, squishing you underneath it, and when you wriggle free, the kittypet is still there. They land a swipe on your face before you can stand up and hiss at you not to be so stupid. When you finally get up and start to leave, you turn around once more to see the kittypet licking the dog's wounds. What a fearless cat!

You step forwards and call out to the dog, attempting to distract it and give the kittypet a chance to escape. When the dog notices, it perks its ears and starts trotting towards you, its tail lashing wildly. You turn and sprint away, but the dog doesn't follow, and you can hear the kittypet laughing as you retreat. You head back to camp to warn your Clan about... whatever that was.

intro_text: While on a lone hunting excursion near the o_c_n border, you hear someone call out your name. On the border sits a o_c_n cat you've been friendly with at Gatherings, and they want to say hello!
decline_text: Feeding your Clan is most important, not conversing with enemy Clans. You leave without a word, leaving your o_c_n friend confused.

success_outcomes.text: You race up to them with a smile, giving their shoulder a friendly head-butt. You sit across the border from each other, sharing news from your Clans, until a bird chirps loudly overhead and you're reminded of your task. Your friend points you in the direction of a full rabbit warren they spotted from across the border, and you thank them as you depart.

It wouldn't hurt to say hello! You prance up to n_c:0 and start up a conversation, which quickly turns into juicy gossip about {PRONOUN/n_c:0/poss} life. {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject/CAP} {VERB/n_c:0/admit/admits} that o_c_n hasn't been treating {PRONOUN/n_c:0/object} well as of late and {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject}{VERB/n_c:0/'re/'s} thinking about leaving. Impulsively, you invite n_c:0 to c_n. With a breath of relief, {PRONOUN/n_c:0/subject} {VERB/n_c:0/accept/accepts} and {VERB/n_c:0/follow/follows} you back to camp.

You greet them from across the border, but before you're able to talk to them, you spot a rabbit from the corner of your eye. You pounce on the rabbit, but it slips out of your paws before you can inflict the killing blow. This happens a few times until you finally successfully catch it. Your friend's laughter is a welcoming sound to your ears.

fail_outcomes.text: You trot up the border, giving the o_c_n cat a warm hello, but before you can get to them, their patrol leader emerges from behind a tree. They scold your friend for getting distracted on their hunt, and you give them a sympathetic nod as they're pulled away.

Even though you would rather talk to your friend from o_c_n, you have to put your duty first. You quickly send them a friendly smile and race off in the other direction, hoping to find a rabbit or two. You have no such luck, and your friends disappointed face doesn't leave your mind.

You invite your friend to walk with you along the border of their Clan, hoping the both of you can still do your duties while talking. It doesn't take long for your friend to find a bird or two for their Clan, but on your side of the border, you're out of luck. The o_c_n cat gives you a sympathetic nod, reluctant to share their prey.

intro_text: Now that newleaf is here, some cats have expressed how nice the camp would look with flower decorations. Do you want to look for some?
decline_text: Flowers would be nice, but you already have a lot to do today. Maybe one of your less-busy Clanmates would be willing to take on the task instead.

success_outcomes.text: The territory is full of flowers, and it's easy to locate some beautiful bunches along your usual patrol routes. You collect a variety and bring them back to camp, earning oohs from some of your Clanmates when they first see the colors. You even have enough to keep some for yourself!

You find a few scattered flowers, but not enough to decorate the entire camp. You switch tactics and collect leaves from the trees that already have them, and trot back to camp with a bristling bundle of them! The bright green really livens up the den entrances.

If you're going to be the one doing the work, then you're going to choose the flowers you like the best! You find a patch of your favorite blossoms just outside of camp, and venture a bit further to find other flowers with colors that match. The camp looks beautiful when you're done with it.

Of course you want to help out, but you don't want to overpick any plants! You spend a whole day combing the territory, carefully picking one or two blossoms at a time. You're exhausted by the end of the day, but you return to camp with a bouquet of dozens of types of flowers. Your Clanmates compliment your effort.

You've been waiting for this moment! You have a plan for the whole camp: what flowers and color schemes to use around the different dens, and how to blend them all together so they look cohesive. You excitedly get to work. The flower collecting and decorating takes several days, but by the end, the camp looks stunning. You're already planning what to do once greenleaf arrives...

fail_outcomes.text: You scour the territory for flowers, but the only ones you can find are medicinal herbs. You collect a few to drop off at the medicine den, and are glad to have helped bolster the herb supply, but are disappointed that the camp still looks as drab as when you started.

You remember seeing a small patch of flowers not far from camp, but when you find it again, it's been mowed down by a group of rabbits. You manage to catch a few rabbits to bring back to camp, though, and your Clanmates are excited to see the unexpected fresh-kill.

You collect some delicate blue flowers at the beginning of a patrol, thinking they would look great around the nursery. Unfortunately, over the course of the patrol they become more wilted and battered, and when you return to camp you're forced to admit they wouldn't make very good decorations anymore.

You're worried about choosing decorations that the rest of your Clan won't like, so you spend a while surveying everyone about their favorite flowers and colors. Unfortunately, everyone has such different preferences that you can't come to a conclusion about what flowers to gather, and you can't help but feel like you've wasted everyone's time.

You head out of camp to pick the most colorful flowers you can find, but you're unable to locate many. When you get back home, you're greeted by new den entrances covered in vibrant plants and shiny stones. Apparently, r_w got to the decorating before you!

intro_text: You're enjoying a meal during a calm, snowy day in camp when suddenly, you receive a sharp and cold thwap to the back of your head. You turn to see plike_r_w_silly with a pawful of snow, snickering to {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/self}.
decline_text: You pick up your fresh-kill and head inside to finish it, ignoring your friend's disappointed face. {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/subject/CAP} should know not to mess with a cat's mealtime!

success_outcomes.text: Challenge accepted! You jump to your paws, dodging your friend's second snowball attack, and manage to tackle {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/object} into a snowbank with a triumphant laugh. You take turns shoving each other into the snow until you're too exhausted to continue.

When you meet plike_r_w_silly's gaze, {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/subject} instantly {VERB/plike_r_w_silly/drop/drops} {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/poss} snowball and {VERB/plike_r_w_silly/dip/dips} {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/poss} head with a polite laugh. Your friend knows you well enough not to continue messing with you during your meal. You'll make sure to get {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/object} back later, though.

fail_outcomes.text: You covertly scoop up a pawful of snow from next to you and turn around, launching it in plike_r_w_silly's direction. Your attack hits {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/object} square in the face-- so hard that {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/subject} {VERB/plike_r_w_silly/let/lets} out a yelp and {VERB/plike_r_w_silly/fall/falls} into the snowbank behind {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/object}. When you rush to check on {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/object}, there's no serious damage and {PRONOUN/plike_r_w_silly/subject} {VERB/plike_r_w_silly/laugh/laughs} it off, but you still feel bad.

intro_text: Dusk brings a surprise: you spot r_w making {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} way to the Moonplace. You're unsure if you're even allowed to communicate with r_w - it's rare for StarClan to speak with anycat other than a leader or medicine cat, but surely ordinary warriors sometimes need guidance from their ancestors, too.
decline_text: Unsure what to do, you call over your fellow StarClan residents to let them know. Some other cat can decide how to respond to r_w.

success_outcomes.text: Just because you can't communicate directly with r_w doesn't mean you can't show {PRONOUN/r_w/object} that StarClan is with {PRONOUN/r_w/object}. When {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/settle/settles} down to unsuccessfully share tongues with StarClan, you gently touch your nose to {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} head, sending {PRONOUN/r_w/object} dreams of warmth and support. When r_w wakes, you can immediately recognize that {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/were/was} reassured that StarClan is always at {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} side, and you purr contentedly to yourself as {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/head/heads} back to camp with a newfound confidence.

You have a feeling that r_w truly needs guidance from StarClan, so you wait for {PRONOUN/r_w/object} to fall asleep at the Moonplace - and you're glad you did. When you greet r_w in the starry landscape, {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/confess/confesses} that {PRONOUN/r_w/subject}{VERB/r_w/'ve/'s} been having strange visions and senses that no other cat seems to have. While you tell {PRONOUN/r_w/object} that you aren't able to explain what these mean, you are able to tell {PRONOUN/r_w/object} that they're messages from StarClan, much like the ones medicine cats and leaders can receive. By the time r_w has to wake up, {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/have/has} a new resolve to speak with the medicine cat, believing {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} latest vision may have been a sign of something the Clan needs to be prepared for.

fail_outcomes.text: There's not much you can do. r_w isn't a leader or medicine cat, and you don't get to talk to just any cat who happens to visit the Moonplace. All you can do is let {PRONOUN/r_w/object} sleep and try to ignore the dismay on {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} face when {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} wake up.

Believing that r_w might truly need some guidance, you decide to give {PRONOUN/r_w/object} the benefit of the doubt. After a barrage of aimless questions, however, it becomes clear that r_w came to the Moonplace out of boredom or curiosity rather than a genuine need to speak with the stars. You cut r_w off midsentence, sharply telling {PRONOUN/r_w/object} that the Moonplace isn't for frivolous things like this, and {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} tail droops in embarrassment as {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/turn/turns} and pads away.

Curious, you decide to wait for r_w on the starry hunting grounds... but {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/don't/doesn't} show up. Certain that the warrior would be asleep by now, you begin searching StarClan's territory, wondering if {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} somehow got lost. Once you reach the border with the Dark Forest, you're about to give up and turn back when something makes your fur stand on end: a whiff of r_w's scent, entangled with the smell of Dark Forest cats.

intro_text: As you wander aimlessly through the Dark Forest, you spot an apprentice from c_n. {PRONOUN/r_a/subject/CAP}{VERB/r_a/'re/'s} alone, ears pinned back as {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} cautiously {VERB/r_a/sniff/sniffs} around the unforgiving landscape. You can't help but wonder if the apprentice has gotten lost.
decline_text: You flick your tail dismissively and continue on your way. If the apprentice can't find {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} way out, why should that be your problem?

success_outcomes.text: With pity for the poor apprentice, you emerge from the shadows, offering to help a startled r_a get out of this frightening place. When {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} {VERB/r_a/accept/accepts}, you tell {PRONOUN/r_a/object} to hold still - and then throw your full weight at the apprentice, slamming into {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} side with your claws sheathed. As {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} form dissipates from the Dark Forest, you feel a prickle of guilt, knowing that r_a will wake up sore... but hey, you got {PRONOUN/r_a/object} to wake up from the nightmare of the Dark Forest, and surely {PRONOUN/r_a/subject}{VERB/r_a/'re/'s} grateful for that.

As you observe r_a trying to find {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} way out of the endless shadows, an idea pops into your head. Making sure you're concealed from sight, you snarl like a coyote - and have to restrain your laughter when the poor apprentice practically leaps out of {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} fur and bolts off. Sure, it was a little mean, but you have to find ways to entertain yourself <i>somehow<i>.

When you step out of the muck and fog, the apprentice whirls to face you, nervously introducing {PRONOUN/r_a/self} as r_a and hesitantly asking if you're from StarClan. Before you can answer, {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} {VERB/r_a/confide/confides} that {PRONOUN/r_a/subject}{VERB/r_a/'re/'s} seeking guidance: {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} {VERB/r_a/worry/worries} that {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} Clanmates see {PRONOUN/r_a/object} as weak, cowardly, and nothing more than an annoyance. Hearing the apprentice's fears, you smile and suggest that {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} train under you. After all, you promise, when {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} {VERB/r_a/demonstrate/demonstrates} what {PRONOUN/r_a/subject}{VERB/r_a/'ve/'s} learned from you, none of {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} Clanmates will doubt what {PRONOUN/r_a/subject}{VERB/r_a/'re/'s} capable of.

fail_outcomes.text: Surely you can show this apprentice how much more powerful {PRONOUN/r_a/subject} can be with the Dark Forest on {PRONOUN/r_a/poss} side! You lift your head, call out to the apprentice, and step forward... and promptly trip over a root, crashing muzzle-first into the muddy ground. There's no way the apprentice will take anything you say seriously now, not with how hard {PRONOUN/r_a/subject}{VERB/r_a/'re/'s} laughing at you.

intro_text: You're bathing yourself the best you can in one of the murky rivers in the Dark Forest, when a starry-pelted kit shows up all of a sudden.
decline_text: You walk away, leaving the kit on its own.

success_outcomes.text: You gently nudge the kit back towards StarClan, telling them that this isn't a place for kits like them. The kit listens, but makes you promise to go play with them someday. You don't have the heart to tell them that's not possible.

fail_outcomes.text: You bare your teeth, trying to startle the kit so that it will run back to the safety of StarClan. However, the kitten doesn't seem very intimidated by you. Instead, it spends some time splashing around in the muck and rolling in the dead grass before wandering back towards the border.

intro_text: While just idly causing minor chaos, you stumble across r_w searching for a cat named l_c. {PRONOUN/r_w/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_w/appear/appears} alone, and suddenly, you feel a large grin creep onto your face. This would be a great opportunity to have some fun!
decline_text: Despite your initial excitement, even you can realize that getting in the way of r_w's search is not appropriate. You'd look for l_c too, if you cared. Oh well!

success_outcomes.text: To stir up some trouble, you appear in r_w's path and claim you know where l_c went, pointing in a random direction. The warrior seems suspicious, but asks again for confirmation. You act like you have no idea what {PRONOUN/r_w/subject}{VERB/r_w/'re/'s} talking about or who {PRONOUN/r_w/subject}{VERB/r_w/'re/'s} searching for, despite clearly knowing. The warrior walks off in frustration.

Once r_w comes close enough to where you're hidden in the shadows, you poke your head out to scare {PRONOUN/r_w/object}. r_w screams like a little mouse and scatters off out of sight, and you can't help just cackle in {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} wake. How hilarious!

Obviously, you have no idea where l_c has gone, but you pop into r_w's line of sight and point in a random direction. Despite you being a Dark Forest cat, r_w seems to take your word for it and thanks you as {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/run/runs} where you guided {PRONOUN/r_w/object} to. Well, r_w is definitely getting lost.

fail_outcomes.text: You had everything right - the talk, attitude, appearance, and jokes, but r_w sees right through you! {PRONOUN/r_w/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_w/continue/continues} on with {PRONOUN/r_w/poss} search, and you're left to your lonesome self. Rats.
You wanted to unnerve r_w, but not this bad! The warrior flees at the sight of you, and you feel a sense of boredom. Couldn't {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} have stayed for at least one joke?

You try your best to stir up some trouble, even claiming you know where l_c went. However, r_w sees through your act. With a hiss, {PRONOUN/r_w/subject} {VERB/r_w/run/runs} off in the opposite direction you pointed to.

intro_text: You scramble your way out from under a bush and glance around. Your plan has worked; you've made it to the world of the living!
decline_text: This is not your place. You turn and dig your way back to the Dark Forest.

success_outcomes.text: As you tentatively walk forward into the c_n camp, it becomes apparent that no cat can actually see you. However, you have a bit of fun passing through their bodies and hearing them remark on the sudden chill.

You walk into the c_n camp and sit at the edge of it, watching the cats go about their day, oblivious to your presence. You dearly miss Clan life. You later return to the Dark Forest with an aching heart.

You expect no one to be able to see you, but as you enter the c_n camp, you lock eyes with r_k. You've spoken with this kit before. {PRONOUN/r_k/subject/CAP}{VERB/r_k/'re/'s} special. You play a quick game of moss-ball with the little fluffball before a queen drags {PRONOUN/r_k/object} away.

You pad into the c_n camp, not expecting anyone to be able to see you. However, as you pass by, plike_r_w lets out a horrified gasp. {PRONOUN/plike_r_w /subject/CAP} can see you, and {PRONOUN/plike_r_w /subject}{VERB/plike_r_w /'re/'s} shocked by where you've ended up. As the muck on your pelt settles into the floor of the camp, you ask plike_r_w if {PRONOUN/plike_r_w /subject}{VERB/plike_r_w /'ve/'s} missed you.

You wander into the c_n camp, where it quickly becomes apparent that no one can see you. That's not a big deal, though. You make your way to the leader's den, where you listen in on some important political discussions between d_n and l_n. Maybe you can visit another Clan's leader in their dreams and tell them about it, just to start some conflict.

fail_outcomes.text: You race forward into the c_n camp, claws unsheathed, ready to give everyone a fright. However, no cat seems to be able to see you. What a waste of a trip.

You step forward to go see your Clan once more, but a paw grabs your haunches and pulls you back. The Dark Forest cat, rdf_c, drags you back to your home, hissing at you about how irresponsible it was to visit the Clan.

intro_text: There's a certain thrill to wandering around areas unknown to you. As you approach the borders of the other afterlives, your curiosity gets to the best of you.
decline_text: They say curiosity kills the cat, and quite frankly, you don't want to die again. So, you swallow back your pride and return to the Unknown Residence.

success_outcomes.text: The clouds of StarClan are surprisingly cool on your paws as you walk through the seemingly endless sea of white. It isn't long until a starry-pelted cat notices your presence, and greets you with a curious look. You explain you just wanted to explore outside of your own afterlife, but they gently crush your curiosity - claiming it's unwise to explore territories without permission. Hearing the warning in their voice, you politely leave.

It didn't take long for a Dark Forest cat to find you walking into {PRONOUN/rdf_c/poss} territory. You exchange greetings with {PRONOUN/rdf_c/object}, and despite thinking rdf_c was going to immediately chase you out, {PRONOUN/rdf_c/subject} {VERB/rdf_c/offer/offers} to teach you about the Dark Forest instead! Before long, you have found a new friend, but regrettably have to leave before a not-so-accepting cat finds you.

fail_outcomes.text: As your paw sinks into the murky depths of the Dark Forest, your heart drops along with it. The unnerving silence, looming trees, and sinking black murk quickly brings you back to reality. What are you doing, trying to explore the Place of No Stars? The disapproving voice of your elders rings in your ears as you slowly creep away.

As you approach StarClan, the sight of endless white clouds is just slightly unnerving, like a poison berry that looks too tasty to resist. Despite knowing in the back of your mind that StarClan is a safe, sacred place, your nerves get to the best of you. Seeing a cat in the far distance, you bolt to where you came from.

There is an uncertainty weighing down heavily on your mind as you cautiously explore the Dark Forest, jumping at every little sound. The deeper you explore, the distant sound of cats fighting draws closer. It's only a matter of time before you get caught, and you're not staying any further to find out.

intro_text: The Dark Forest has always been a bit...dull. This is why you, in your long-standing effort to try and restore a little bit of life into the gloomy forest, are making your way through the dark forest with a muck-soaked ball of moss held tightly in your jaws. You're eager to check on the progress of the sparkling blue flower that you have managed to sneak over from StarClan's hunting grounds.
decline_text: On second thought, maybe you should just leave it alone for a little while.

success_outcomes.text: A soft glow greets you as you pad into the little area that you had cleared out. Against all odds, the flower is still alive! You carefully squeeze the contents of the moss onto the soil, mind racing as you start thinking about the possibility of giving a bit of life to the Dark Forest.

fail_outcomes.text: The path feels longer than you remember... Wait, didn't you pass that tree earlier? You wander around for a little longer until you accept that you're hopelessly lost. Looks like you have to go through the trouble of sneaking in another flower. Great.

The lack of sunlight, or anything in the Dark Forest for that matter, doesn't mix well with plants. You learn that with a sinking heart as you are met with a dead, wilting flower slumped onto the soil.

Your hopes are immediately squashed as you find the shredded, muddy remnants of the flower littering the ground, accompanied by deep claw marks in the soil and muddy pawprints that lead away from the scene. Not everyone seems to be a fan of your efforts.

intro_text: You are wandering around aimlessly, deep in thought, when a shadow in the distance grabs your attention. You watch as the shadow turns, its tail flicking up in a greeting. A voice soon follows, calling out to you.
decline_text: You're not in the mood for a conversation. You slink away into a cloud of mist, ignoring as the stranger's cries grow shrill and desperate, eventually fading off into the distance.

success_outcomes.text: Intrigued, you venture closer. You step out of the mist to find you nose-to-nose with another cat! They explain that they are a kittypet who has gotten lost while on an adventure out of their garden, eagerly asking if you knew where their home was. You feel a twinge of pity as you gently explain that this is the afterlife, and that they likely died while on their adventure. The kittypet sits in stunned silence for a while, then quietly thanks you as they stand up and stumble off into the mist.

fail_outcomes.text: You make your way towards the shadow, stepping out of a cloud of mist to find... nothing. You look around, but the shadow is nowhere to be seen, leaving you in an eerie silence. You shiver and the fur along your spine ripples with unease as you quickly scurry back into a cloud of mist.

You try to make your way towards the voice, but you quickly lose yourself in a cloud of mist. You wander in circles, trying to pinpoint the stranger's increasingly desperate cries until they mysteriously fade into silence.

intro_text: As you wander the endless lush territory of StarClan, you find yourself searching for herbs in similar spots to where you did in life.
decline_text: You cut the herb search short; it's not like there's much you can do with them in StarClan, anyway.

success_outcomes.text: Your muscle memory leads you through a path in the woods and to the base of a tree. On the underside of a large, twisted root, is a well-hidden patch of plantain. You're halfway through picking it when you remember that cats don't get injured or sick in StarClan, so they won't have any use back at camp. Oh, well. You leave the herb bundle there... maybe some StarClan squirrels can make use of it.

You catch a familiar scent and snake your way through the trees until you find it-- a large, untouched patch of catmint surrounding a crystal-clear puddle. Looking into the puddle gives you a hazy view of the c_n medicine den! You pluck a few catmint leaves and drop them in, hoping your living Clanmates will be able to receive them, and take the remaining herbs back to your StarClan camp.
Your herb-finding instincts lead you to a beautiful patch of berry bushes deep in StarClan territory. You're able to wrestle a branch from one of the bushes to bring the kits a nice snack.

You catch an herb scent on the breeze and follow it until you stumble across the c_n medicine cat, r_m, in the middle of a dream! You have nothing profound to say... something tells you you aren't supposed to be here. You just give r_m a wise nod before turning and running back to camp. How in, well, here {VERB/r_m/are/is} {PRONOUN/r_m/subject} going to end up interpreting that?

fail_outcomes.text: You follow your instincts to some familiar-looking territory, only to find that the mullein patch you were expecting to find isn't there. It makes sense, you suppose... herbs aren't really necessary in StarClan. But it's still a bit disappointing.
You catch a familiar herb scent and follow it into the wilderness, but you end up traipsing in circles. Perhaps you were just imagining it.
